LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SARIT SIMHAYOFF-COHEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. IN RULING ON A MOTION TO STRIKE, THE ALLEGATIONS OF A COMPLAINT ARE CONSIDERED AS A WHOLE AND ARE PRESUMED TO BE TRUE. (CLAUSON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 67 CAL.APP.4TH 1253, 1255.) IN ORDER TO SURVIVE A MOTION TO STRIKE AN ALLEGATION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING AN ENTITLEMENT TO SUCH RELIEF MUST BE PLED BY A PLAINTIFF. (GRIEVES V. SUPERIOR COURT (1984) 157 CAL.APP.3D 159, 166.) PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS ARGREED THAT PLAINTIFF'S $48,150 DEPOSIT WAS REFUNDABLE. THE FACTS THAT PLAINTIFF ALLEGES IN SUPPORT OF HIS CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION SUPPORT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. COURT TO REVIEW PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

Plaintiff'S Motion For Award Of Reasonable Expenses Under C.C.P. Section 415.30
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert P. Travis, of Travis & Travis, Tel. 415.923.1200, for plaintiffs K. Brian Romaneschi;
Jonathan J. Belaga, of Robbins, Fettner & LemMon, Tel. 510.451.7128, for defendant Dan Wiskind.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Plaintiff'S Motion For Award Of Reasonable Expenses Under C.C.P. Section 415.30 is granted in part and defendant Dan Wiskind's request for monetary sanctions per CCP 128.5 is denied. Mr. Wiskind is required to pay Mr. Romaneschi $2585, consisting of the e-filing and newspaper charges incurred by Mr. Romenschi in serving Mr. Wiskind by publication but excluding attorney's fees. While there is no case law on point, the better reading of the phrase "reasonable expenses" in section 415.30(d) excludes attorney's fees. (See Weil and Brown book at 4:238-243 (equating expenses with costs)). Although Mr. Wiskind may not have personally received the service packet when it was mailed to the Sutter Street address, the court finds that any failure by him to receive the packet was due to his deliberate decision not to receive mail that appeared to be sent by an attorney. Under these circumstances, section 415.30(d) mandates an award to Mr. Romaneschi for reasonable expenses incurred in serving Mr. Wiskind. The efiling and newspaper charges were reasonably incurred and are thus recoverable. Order signed. The Court stays entry of the order until March 16, 2017, per counsel's request to appeal the ruling. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEFENDANT DAN WISKIND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternative motion for SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES are DENIED in their entirety. There are triable issues as to alter ego liability as to Mr. Wiskind which precludes summary resolution of all of Mr. Ronaneschi's claims. "There is no litmus test to determine when the corporate veil will be pierced; rather the result will depend on the circumstances of each particular case. There are, nevertheless, two general requirements: '(1) that there be such unity of interest and ownership that the separate personalities of the corporation and the individual no longer exist and (2) that, if the acts are treated as those of the corporation alone, an inequitable result will follow.'" (Mesler v. Bragg Management Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 300 (quoting Automotriz etc. de California v. Resnick (1957) 47 Cal.2d 792, 796).) As to all five causes of action, there are triable issues regarding both requirements. Liberally construing the evidence submitted in the opposition papers, Mr. Romaneschi has shown that Mr. Wiskind admitted he was at some point an owner of World of Charms but ceased being idnentified as a partner/owner so he could show less income and discourage creditors. (Response to UMF 3; Isabel Wiskind Declaration par. 8; Isabel Wiskind Deposition 31:2-9.). Written order is signed and filed in open court.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SARIT SIMHAYOFF-COHEN'S Motion To Strike is DENIED. In ruling on a motion to strike, the allegations of a complaint are considered as a whole and are presumed to be true. (Clauson v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255.) In order to survive a motion to strike an allegation of punitive damages, the ultimate facts showing an entitlement to such relief must be pled by a plaintiff. (Grieves v. The facts that Plaintiff alleges in support of his claim for negligent misrepresentation support Plaintiff's claims for punitive damages. Court to revies proposed order submitted by Plaintiff. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert P. Travis, of Travis & Travis, Tel. 415.923.1200, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT SARIT SIMHAYOFF-COHEN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. CCP sec. 412.20 governs the content of a summons directed at an individual defendant. There is no requirement in section 412.20 that a summons explicitly states the name of the individual defendant being served with the summons; rather, the only requirement is that the summons states the names of the parties in the action, which the summons in this case did. (Declaration of Sarit Simhayoff Cohen, Ex. A.) Additionally, CCP sec. 412.20(c) provides that "[a] summons in a form approved by the Judicial Council is deemed to comply with this section." Plaintiff used a Judicial Council Form Summons. (Cohen Decl., Ex. A.) The Judicial Council Form only requires the plaintiff to check a box stating that "You are being served as an individual defendant." There is no requirement on the Judicial Council Form that the individual defendant be identified by name next to where the box is checked. Further, Mannesmann Demag, Ltd. v. Sup. Ct. (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 1118, 1122, cited by Defendant, is inapplicable. That case discussed CCP sec. 420.30, which explicitly requires that a summons identify a corporate defendant by name. Order signed in open court. Judge Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/JMQ)
